attacking_style: Attacks solo from established breakaways before final climbs, forcing premature reactions from stronger riders. Prefers initiating moves 20-30km out to build gaps before key terrain.
team_tactics: Operates with significant autonomy in breakaways as experienced diesel engine. Minimal team setup required; relies on individual positioning and break management rather than domestique support.
terrain_preference: Long-distance breakaways on transitional stages with circuit racing or multi-climb profiles. Thrives in scenarios requiring sustained diesel efforts over 100km+ before selective finales.
vulnerability: Poker tactics in breaks can backfire when companions refuse cooperation. Age-related decline limits ability to follow explosive accelerations on steep climbs after long efforts.
key_patterns: Attacks solo from established breaks before final obstacles, documented at Giro and Dauphiné (2 instances)
Sustains long breakaway efforts 6+ hours, viable diesel engine for marathon breaks (2 instances)
Tactical experience forces premature GC rider responses, disrupting chase organization (2 instances)
Operates as break spoiler/aggressor rather than waiting for final sprint (2 instances)
form_trajectory: negative: Aging diesel (35+ years) still functions for long breaks but tactical gambles increasingly backfire; diminishing returns on experience versus declining physical capacity
